title: Variables should not be checked against the values they're about to be assigned
description: Variables should not be checked against the values they're about to be assignedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEconfusingWhy is this an issue?There’s no point in checking a variable against the value you’re about to assign it. Save the cycles and lines of code, and simply perform the assignment.Noncompliant code exampleif (x != a) // Noncompliant; why bother? { x = a; }Compliant solutionx = a;ExceptionsProperties and checks inside setters are excluded from this rule because they could have side effects and removing the check could lead to undesired side effects.if (MyProperty != a) { MyProperty = a; // Compliant because the setter could be expensive call }private int myField; public int SomeProperty { get
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects if-statements that check a variable against a value before assigning that same value

checks.message: Redundant check before assignment - just assign directly
Redundant check before assignment - just assign directly

logic.triggering_conditions: if (x != value) { x = value; } OR if (x != value) x = value;
logic.exclusion_conditions: Property assignments (may have side effects), member access patterns
never_trigger_on: Property assignments (setter may have side effects)
logic.reasoning: Checking a variable against a value before assigning that value is redundant for simple variables. The assignment itself is sufficient. Exception: property setters may have side effects that make the check worthwhile.
